More frequent monitoring if family history and clinical markers present.
NICE CG185, CANMAT 2018 — separate algorithm for cyclothymic disorder is limited; bipolar spectrum principles apply; psychotherapy first-line.
Interpersonal and Social Rhythm Therapy (IPSRT) - Frank (Frank E.)
Circadian rhythm and interpersonal stress management.
Patient's documentation of own mood changes — diagnostic and therapeutic tool.

Functional level improves with adequate treatment.
Myth 1: "Cyclothymic temperament is a normal personality trait, does not require treatment"

cyclothymic temperament when it is a normal variant has no functional impairment; clinical diagnosis requires functional impairment. However, many patients have explained it as a 'creative nature' for years and do not seek intervention, while quality of life deteriorates.
Myth 2: "Cyclothymic disorder will not convert to bipolar"
Akiskal — 15–50% conversion to bipolar Type I or II; higher risk if family history.
Myth 3: "Antidepressant alone manages all mood changes"
Manic switching and rapid cycling risk; required concomitant with mood stabilizer.
Myth 4: "A cyclothymic patient will lose their creativity due to treatment"
appropriate treatment (psychotherapy + mood stabilizer) helps preserve and enhance creativity (stabilized activity); romanticized 'duality' explanation leads to avoidance of intervention.
Myth 5: "Herbal preparations or diet cure cyclothymic disorder"
St John's Wort risk of manic switch; omega-3 mild adjunct, not primary treatment.
Yatham L.N. et al. CANMAT/ISBD 2018. Bipolar Disord 2018;20(2):97–170.
